notes: ASIC Crowd-Sourced Funding compliance rules. Real rule set will be extracted from RG 261 and RG 262 markdown by parallel agents. This file seeds the engine with 5 sample rules for end-to-end validation.

rules.claim: Marketing must direct readers to the offer document and general CSF risk warning per s738ZG(6)
required_phrase_patterns: Always consider the general CSF risk warning
rules.violation_message: Add 'Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.' to the bottom of the content.

rules.claim: Marketing copy must not state specific investment dollar amounts (deposit, minimum, maximum) outside of the offer document
rules.violation_message: Remove specific investment $ amounts. Use generic phrasing like 'small refundable deposit' or 'low minimum entry'.

rules.claim: CSF offers must not be solicited via DM/email reply/phone — anti-hawking under s736
rules.violation_message: Remove. Investments must go via the licensed CSF intermediary (e.g. Birchal), not via direct contact.

rules.claim: Forward-looking statements (forecasts, projections, growth targets) must have stated reasonable grounds; speculative claims are misleading
rules.llm_prompt: Read this marketing copy. Does it contain any forward-looking financial statement (forecast, growth target, expected return, projected revenue, future valuation, profitability projection) WITHOUT stating supporting assumptions, basis, or evidence? Return JSON: {"pass": <bool>, "evidence": "<exact quote if fail>", "reason": "<brief explanation>"}
rules.violation_message: Either add the basis/assumptions for the forward-looking claim, or remove it.

rules.claim: Superseded ASIC RG 261 disclaimer wording — replaced by founder-directed CSF warning 2026-04-25
forbidden_patterns: This is not financial advice\. Consider seeking independent financial advice\. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal
rules.violation_message: Replace with the canonical CSF warning: 'Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.'
